title: Portfolio Dashboard
description: A focused dashboard concept for presenting projects, metrics, and portfolio content in a clear operational view.
summary: A dashboard-style portfolio system for presenting projects, content, and useful signals in one structured interface.

sections.heading: Overview
body: This project explores how a portfolio can behave more like a focused dashboard than a static gallery. The goal is to show projects, writing, skills, and availability in a way that is easy to scan.
The structure is designed for recruiters, HR reviewers, and freelance clients who need to understand what I can build quickly.

sections.heading: Challenge
body: Portfolio pages often become visual showcases without enough operational context. For a fullstack profile, the challenge is showing both the interface quality and the practical thinking behind each project.
The layout needed to stay direct, readable, and easy to extend as new work is added.

sections.heading: Approach
body: I organized the experience around reusable content blocks, consistent metadata, and clear project summaries. Each section is built to support bilingual content and future real case studies.
The interface keeps the current visual style while moving the content source into editable data files.

sections.heading: Outcome
body: The result is a flexible portfolio foundation that can grow from placeholders into real case studies without changing the page architecture.
It gives future updates a clear path: edit content files, replace assets, and keep the presentation consistent.
